evals.name: gzip-rfc-1952-tier-1
evals.prompt: I want to write a Go encoder/decoder for the gzip file format and I'd like a clean reference to implement against. Pull the binary format spec from RFC 1952 (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1952.txt) into ./gzip-spec/. I care about the member header, the FLG bit field, the optional extra fields (FEXTRA, FNAME, FCOMMENT, FHCRC), and the trailer (CRC32 + ISIZE). I don't need the deflate compressed-data details — just the framing.
evals.expected_output: Directory ./gzip-spec/ containing SPEC.md (overview, conventions, indexes), structures/ with files for the gzip member header, optional extra fields, and trailer; encoding-tables/ for OS values and the FLG bit field; examples/ with at least minimal/typical/complex worked examples.

assertions.text: SPEC.md exists at the root of the output directory
structures/ directory exists and contains at least 2 .md files
examples/ directory exists and contains at least 3 .md files (minimal/typical/complex or equivalent)
every .md file produced is non-empty (at least 200 bytes)
no leftover scratch files (_spec.txt, _spec.html, _toc.md, _structures_index.md) in the output directory
SPEC.md contains a 'Conventions' section that states byte order explicitly
SPEC.md identifies gzip as little-endian (or LSB-first / Intel byte order)
SPEC.md contains a 'Structures index' section linking to each structures/<name>.md file
every structures/<name>.md file contains a markdown field table with Offset / Size / Type / Name / Description columns
field tables use Go-friendly types (uint8, uint16, uint32, [N]byte, []byte)
the FLG byte's bit fields are documented (FTEXT, FHCRC, FEXTRA, FNAME, FCOMMENT) with bit numbers
the gzip magic identifier (ID1=0x1f, ID2=0x8b) is captured
a structure file describes the trailer with CRC32 (4 bytes) and ISIZE (4 bytes)
an encoding table for OS values exists (or OS values are documented inline with at least 5 entries)
FEXTRA, FNAME, FCOMMENT, FHCRC are documented as conditional/optional, with their presence tied to FLG bits
the compressed-data block (DEFLATE format) is not extracted as a structure (user explicitly excluded it)

evals.name: dns-rfc-1035-tier-2
evals.prompt: Extract the DNS message binary format from RFC 1035 (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc1035.txt) into ./dns-spec/ so I can implement a Go DNS message encoder/decoder. I'm focused on section 4 (Messages): the 12-byte header, the question section, and resource records — including the bit-packed flags in the header and label compression in domain names. Skip the master file format (zone files) and the operational/registry stuff in earlier sections.
evals.expected_output: Directory ./dns-spec/ with SPEC.md, structures/ for header/question/resource-record/domain-name (label format + compression), encoding-tables/ for opcodes, response codes, query types, query classes, RR types, and RR classes, plus minimal/typical/complex examples.

assertions.text: SPEC.md exists at the root of the output directory
structures/ directory exists and contains at least 3 .md files (header, question, resource-record at minimum)
encoding-tables/ directory exists and contains at least 4 .md files
examples/ directory exists and contains at least 3 .md files
every .md file produced is non-empty (at least 200 bytes)
no leftover scratch files (_spec.txt, _spec.html, _toc.md, _structures_index.md) in the output directory
SPEC.md contains a 'Conventions' section that states byte order explicitly
SPEC.md identifies DNS as network byte order / big-endian
SPEC.md contains a 'Structures index' section linking to each structures/<name>.md file
every structures/<name>.md file contains a markdown field table with Offset / Size / Type / Name / Description columns
field tables use Go-friendly types (uint8, uint16, uint32, [N]byte, []byte)
the DNS header structure documents 12 bytes: ID (2), Flags (2), QDCOUNT (2), ANCOUNT (2), NSCOUNT (2), ARCOUNT (2)
the DNS header's flags bytes have a bit-fields section covering QR, Opcode (4 bits), AA, TC, RD, RA, Z, RCODE — with correct bit positions
domain name encoding documents both the length-prefixed label format and the compression pointer format (two high bits = 11, 14-bit offset)
encoding tables exist for opcodes, RCODEs (response codes), TYPE / QTYPE values, and CLASS / QCLASS values
the resource record structure documents NAME, TYPE, CLASS, TTL, RDLENGTH, RDATA fields
the master file format (zone file syntax) is NOT extracted as part of the binary spec — user explicitly excluded it

evals.name: png-rfc-2083-tier-2
evals.prompt: I'm building a Go PNG decoder and need a structured spec to work from. Use RFC 2083 (https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2083.txt) and produce a reference at ./png-spec/. Cover the file signature, the chunk layout (Length / Type / Data / CRC), and at minimum the critical chunks: IHDR, PLTE, IDAT, IEND. The ancillary chunks (tRNS, gAMA, etc.) can each get their own structure file too. Be precise about CRC scope, byte order, and the chunk-type ASCII case bits.
evals.expected_output: Directory ./png-spec/ with SPEC.md (network byte order, chunk layout overview), structures/ for png-signature, chunk-frame, chunk-ihdr, chunk-plte, chunk-idat, chunk-iend, plus ancillary chunks (one file each), encoding-tables/ for color types and chunk-type case bits, plus minimal/typical/complex examples.
